tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: And having raised up your dead.
sm: and having raised your dead.

syn.mc: Coordinate participial phrase completing the identification begun in lines 669-670.
syn.svo: Participle: ἀναστήσας, modifying οὗτός from line 669. Object: τοὺς νεκρούς σου.
syn.ptc: ἀναστήσας is coordinate with θεραπεύσας in line 670.
syn.rhet: Healing the sick and raising the dead crown the benefactions attributed to the coming one.

lit: This is he against whom you dared;
sm: This is the one against whom you dared to act;

syn.mc: Main identificatory clause.
syn.sub: Relative clause εἰς ὃν ἐτόλμησας qualifies οὗτός.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Relative phrase: εἰς ὃν ἐτόλμησας.
syn.rhet: First member of a pounding anaphoric series: οὗτός ἐστιν.

lit: This is he against whom you acted impiously;
sm: This is the one against whom you committed impiety;

syn.mc: Second identificatory clause in the series.
syn.sub: Relative clause εἰς ὃν ἠσέβησας qualifies οὗτός.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Relative clause verb: ἠσέβησας.
syn.rhet: The repeated frame intensifies the accusation by varying the offense.

lit: This is he against whom you acted unjustly;
sm: This is the one whom you wronged;

syn.mc: Third identificatory clause in the series.
syn.sub: Relative clause εἰς ὃν ἠδίκησας qualifies οὗτός.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Relative clause verb: ἠδίκησας.
syn.rhet: The anaphora drives home cumulative guilt.

lit: This is he whom you killed;
sm: This is the one whom you killed;

syn.mc: Fourth identificatory clause.
syn.sub: Relative clause ὃν ἀπέκτεινας qualifies οὗτός.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Relative clause verb: ἀπέκτεινας with object ὃν.
syn.rhet: The series reaches explicit homicide.

lit: This is he whom you <UNCERTAIN: sold for silver?>
sm: This is the one whom you <UNCERTAIN: treated as silver-paid / sold for silver>,
emd: <UNCERTAIN: ἠργυρίσω is lexically difficult but not securely corrigible from the local evidence; preserved as transmitted.>

syn.mc: Fifth identificatory clause, continued by the participial elaboration in line 679.
syn.sub: Relative clause ὃν ἠργυρίσω qualifies οὗτός.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Relative clause centers on the difficult transmitted verb ἠργυρίσω.
syn.rhet: The series shifts from violence to monetary degradation.

src: ἀπαιτήσας παρ᾽ αὐτοῦ τὸ δίδραχμα ὑπὲρ τῆς κεφαλῆς αὐτοῦ.
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: Having demanded from him the didrachm for his head.
sm: after exacting from him the didrachm for his own head.

syn.mc: Circumstantial participial phrase elaborating the charge of line 678.
syn.svo: Participle: ἀπαιτήσας, modifying the implied second-person subject of ἠργυρίσω. Object: τὸ δίδραχμα. Source phrase: παρ᾽ αὐτοῦ. Reference phrase: ὑπὲρ τῆς κεφαλῆς αὐτοῦ.
syn.ptc: ἀπαιτήσας explains how the monetary insult was enacted.
syn.rhet: The detail adds mercenary pettiness to the indictment.

src: Aχάριστε ᾿Ισραήλ, δεῦρο καὶ κρίθητι πρὸς ἐμὲ
ana: Ἀχάριστε Ἰσραήλ, δεῦρο καὶ κρίθητι πρὸς ἐμὲ
lit: Ungrateful Israel, come here and be judged with me
sm: Ungrateful Israel, come and answer before me,
emd: Normalized mixed Latin/Greek A in Aχάριστε and removed the stray spacing psili from ᾿Ισραήλ.

syn.mc: Direct summons and imperative challenge.
syn.svo: Vocative: Ἀχάριστε Ἰσραήλ. Imperatives: δεῦρο, κρίθητι. Prepositional phrase: πρὸς ἐμέ.
syn.rhet: A judicial summons opens the formal indictment for ingratitude.

lit: Concerning your ingratitude.
sm: about your ingratitude.

syn.mc: Prepositional phrase completing the summons of line 681.
syn.svo: The phrase περὶ τῆς ἀχαριστίας σου depends on κρίθητι.
syn.rhet: The charge is named explicitly before the catalogue of benefits.

src: πόσου ἀνετιμήσω τὸ ὑπ᾽ αὐτοῦ πλασθῆναι;
lit: For how much shall I value being formed by him?
sm: What price can I set on your being formed by him?

syn.mc: Direct rhetorical question beginning the catalogue of benefits.
syn.sub: The articular infinitive τὸ ... πλασθῆναι serves as the object of valuation.
syn.svo: Interrogative genitive of price: πόσου. Verb: ἀνετιμήσω. Object: τὸ ὑπ᾽ αὐτοῦ πλασθῆναι.
syn.rhet: Anaphoric πόσου ἀνετιμήσω begins a crescendo of unpayable benefactions.

src: πόσου ἀνετιμήσω τὴν τῶν πατέρων σου ἀνεύρεσιν;
lit: For how much shall I value the finding of your fathers?
sm: What price can I put on the finding of your fathers?

syn.mc: Second rhetorical valuation question.
syn.svo: Interrogative genitive: πόσου. Verb: ἀνετιμήσω. Object: τὴν τῶν πατέρων σου ἀνεύρεσιν.
syn.rhet: The repeated formula expands the scope from creation to ancestral history.

src: πόσου ἀνετιμήσω τὴν εἰς Αἴγυπτον κάθοδον
lit: For how much shall I value the descent into Egypt
sm: What price can I put on the descent into Egypt

syn.mc: Third rhetorical valuation question, continued in line 686.
syn.svo: Interrogative genitive: πόσου. Verb: ἀνετιμήσω. Object: τὴν ... κάθοδον.
syn.rhet: The catalogue moves into the Exodus story's prehistory.

src: καὶ τὴν ἐκεῖ διατροφὴν διὰ τοῦ καλοῦ ᾿Ιωσήφ;
ana: καὶ τὴν ἐκεῖ διατροφὴν διὰ τοῦ καλοῦ Ἰωσήφ;
lit: And the sustenance there through good Joseph?
sm: and the sustenance there through noble Joseph?
emd: Removed the stray spacing psili from ᾿Ιωσήφ.

syn.mc: Coordinate continuation of the valuation question begun in line 685.
syn.svo: The governing verb remains ἀνετιμήσω from line 685. Added object: τὴν ἐκεῖ διατροφήν. Means phrase: διὰ τοῦ καλοῦ Ἰωσήφ.
syn.rhet: Joseph is recalled as providential mediator of preservation.

lit: For how much shall I value the ten plagues?
sm: What price can I assign to the ten plagues?

syn.mc: Another rhetorical valuation question.
syn.svo: Interrogative genitive: πόσου. Verb: ἀνετιμήσω. Object: τὰς δέκα πληγάς.
syn.rhet: The rescue of Israel is recalled through Egypt's judgments.

lit: For how much shall I value the nightly pillar
sm: What price can I put on the pillar of night

syn.mc: Rhetorical question continued through lines 689-690.
syn.svo: Interrogative genitive: πόσου. Verb: ἀνετιμήσω. Object begins with τὸν νυκτερινὸν στῦλον.
syn.rhet: The Exodus guidance motifs are unfolded in sequence.

lit: And the daytime cloud
sm: and the cloud by day

syn.mc: Coordinate continuation of the valuation question begun in line 688.
syn.svo: The governing verb remains ἀνετιμήσω from line 688. Added object: τὴν ἡμερινὴν νεφέλην.
syn.rhet: Night and day are paired in balanced parallel.

lit: And the crossing through the Red Sea?
sm: and the crossing through the Red Sea?
emd: Removed the stray spacing psili from ᾿Ερυθρᾶς.

syn.mc: Coordinate continuation of the question begun in line 688.
syn.svo: The governing verb remains ἀνετιμήσω. Added object: τὴν ... διάβασιν.
syn.rhet: The climactic crossing caps the wilderness-guidance triad.

src: πόσου ἀνετιμήσω τὴν ἐξ οὐρανοῦ μαννοδοσίαν
lit: For how much shall I value the manna-gift from heaven
sm: What price can I put on the manna from heaven

syn.mc: New rhetorical valuation question, continued through lines 692-695.
syn.svo: Interrogative genitive: πόσου. Verb: ἀνετιμήσω. Object begins with τὴν ... μαννοδοσίαν.
syn.rhet: Provision in the wilderness replaces deliverance motifs in the catalogue.

lit: And water-supply from a rock
sm: and water from the rock

syn.mc: Coordinate continuation of the question begun in line 691.
syn.svo: The governing verb remains ἀνετιμήσω. Added object: ὑδροπαροχίαν with source phrase ἐκ πέτρας.
syn.rhet: The benefactions are named in concise, almost liturgical noun phrases.

lit: And legislation in Horeb
sm: and the giving of the law at Horeb

syn.mc: Coordinate continuation of the question begun in line 691.
syn.svo: The governing verb remains ἀνετιμήσω. Added object: νομοθεσίαν with locative ἐν Χωρὴβ.
syn.rhet: Provision yields to covenantal gift.

lit: And the inheritance of the land
sm: and the inheritance of the land

syn.mc: Coordinate continuation of the question begun in line 691.
syn.svo: The governing verb remains ἀνετιμήσω. Added object: τὴν γῆς κληρονομίαν.
syn.rhet: The list moves from journey to possession.

lit: And the gifts there?
sm: and the gifts granted there?

syn.mc: Final coordinate member of the question begun in line 691.
syn.svo: The governing verb remains ἀνετιμήσω. Added object: τὰς ἐκεῖ δωρεάς.
syn.rhet: The open-ended plural broadens the catalogue beyond named items.

tok: pres act ptcp acc pl m subst
lit: For how much shall I value the ones suffering
sm: What price can I put on the sufferers

syn.mc: Rhetorical valuation question continued in line 697.
syn.sub: The relative clause follows in line 697.
syn.svo: Interrogative genitive: πόσου. Verb: ἀνετιμήσω. Object: τοὺς πάσχοντας.
syn.ptc: πάσχοντας is substantival: 'those who were suffering'.
syn.rhet: The catalogue turns from historical gifts to Christ's acts of healing.

tok: pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: Whom he himself, being present, healed?
sm: whom he himself healed in person?

syn.mc: Relative clause completing the object of line 696.
syn.svo: Object: οὗς. Subject: αὐτός. Verb: ἐθεράπευσεν. Participle: παρών.
syn.ptc: παρὼν stresses personal presence in the act of healing.
syn.rhet: The relative clause intensifies immediacy: he healed them himself, in person.

lit: Value for me the withered hand
sm: Set a value for me on the withered hand

syn.mc: Direct imperative challenge, continued in line 699.
syn.sub: The relative clause follows in line 699.
syn.svo: Verb: τίμησαί. Dative of interest: μοι. Object: τὴν ξηρὰν χεῖρα.
syn.rhet: The catalogue shifts from questions to taunting imperatives of valuation.

lit: Which he restored to the body
sm: which he restored to the body,

syn.mc: Relative clause completing the object of line 698.
syn.sub: Relative clause qualifies τὴν ξηρὰν χεῖρα.
syn.svo: Object: ἣν. Subject: implied αὐτός / Christ. Verb: ἀπεκατέστησεν. Dative complement: τῷ σώματι.
syn.rhet: The miracle is compressed into a single restorative verb.

src: τίμησαΐ μοι τοὺς ἐϊς γενετῆς τυφλοὺς
ana: τίμησαί μοι τοὺς ἐκ γενετῆς τυφλοὺς
lit: Value for me the blind from birth
sm: Set a value for me on the blind from birth
emd: Followed change_log.txt: τίμησαΐ -> τίμησαί and ἐϊς γενετῆς -> ἐκ γενετῆς.

syn.mc: Direct imperative challenge continued by the relative clause in line 701.
syn.sub: The qualifying relative clause begins in the next line.
syn.svo: Verb: τίμησαί. Dative of interest: μοι. Object: τοὺς ἐκ γενετῆς τυφλούς.
syn.rhet: The taunting imperative continues with another healing miracle as evidence.
